latest.title: Exercise of Warrants
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 11:57
RNS Number : 6265W
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
("Borders & Southern" or the "Company")
Exercise of Warrants
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
(AIM: BOR), the
London
based independent oil and gas exploration company with appraisal and exploration assets offshore the
Falkland Islands, announces that it has received notification from Will Hodson, a Non-Executive Director, to exercise warrants over 105,263 new ordinary shares of
1 pence
each in the capital of the Company ("Warrant Shares") at the exercise price of
10 pence
per Warrant Share, for an aggregate cash value of
£10,526.30.
Application has been made for admission of the Warrant Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission") and it is expected that Admission will occur on or around 19 March 2026. The Warrant Shares will rank pari passu with the existing Ordinary Shares of 1 pence each in the capital of the Company.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will consist of 879,420,674 ordinary shares each with voting rights. The Company does not hold any ordinary shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 879,420,674 and this is the figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please visit
www.bordersandsouthern.com
or contact:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
Tel: 020 7071 6984
Harry Baker, CEO

Notes to Editors:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc is an oil & gas exploration company listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (BOR). The Company operates and has a 100% interest in three Production Licences in the South Falkland Basin covering an area of nearly 10,000 square kilometres. The Company has acquired 2,517 square kilometres of 3D seismic and drilled two exploration wells, making a significant gas condensate discovery with its first well.
-ends-

latest.title: Sea Lion FID Announcement
latest.content:
10 Dec 2025 13:04
RNS Number : 0518L
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
10 December 2025
10 December 2025
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
("Borders & Southern" or the "Company")
Sea Lion FID Announcement
Borders & Southern (AIM: BOR), a London based independent oil and gas exploration company with appraisal and exploration assets offshore the Falkland Islands, notes the announcement by Rockhopper Exploration ("Rockhopper") this morning in which operator Navitas Petroleum and Rockhopper confirmed that a final investment decision ("FID") for the Sea Lion development project in the North Falkland Basin, offshore the Falkland Islands, has been taken.
Harry Baker, CEO of Borders and Southern, commented:
"We are delighted that Navitas and partner, Rockhopper, have taken FID on Sea Lion. It is a transformational moment for all stakeholders in the Falkland Islands. The significance of the region taking its first step towards being a fully-fledged oil and gas producing province cannot be overestimated. FID on Sea Lion proves that projects in the Falklands are bankable and further enhances the value of our project.
We believe that FID on Sea Lion as well as the opening up of a new oil province will re-focus attention from both the oil industry and its investors on the area and Darwin. Our world class Darwin prospect has over 460 million barrels of recoverable liquids, a phased development plan, very quick payback and huge upside with 40 plus prospects that could yield over 9 billion barrels of liquid hydrocarbons and 10Tcf of gas. We hold 100% of all our licenses. Borders & Southern look forward to updating the market as we continue to progress our Darwin development.
Borders & Southern are delighted for Sam Moody and his team at Rockhopper in bringing the Sea Lion project on this journey from concept to development."
For further information please visit
www.bordersandsouthern.com
or contact:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc

company_30s.what_they_do: BOR.L operates in the energy space, concentrating on LNG supply and refining activities.  The company is working on the Sea Lion project, a mid‑scale LNG facility that aims to tap growing demand for cleaner gas.
company_30s.why_they_matter: LNG is a key transition fuel, and the UK’s gas market is tightening.  A small, nimble player that can deliver a new terminal quickly has a clear niche against larger, slower incumbents.  The Sea Lion project could give BOR.L a foothold in a market that is still expanding.
company_30s.current_state: BOR.L is still in a pre‑revenue, high‑growth phase.  Recent actions – director share purchases, warrant exercises and a formal FID – signal that insiders are betting on the project and that the company is moving from planning into execution.

rns_narrative: Capital moves: Directors have bought shares and exercised warrants, injecting fresh equity and showing confidence.
Project milestone: The Sea Lion terminal received a formal Feasibility ID (FID), moving the company from study to construction.
Governance & reporting: Regular AGM and half‑year updates keep shareholders informed and demonstrate transparency.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is largely silent but a few voices highlight the capital boost and project approval.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the fresh equity and the FID as evidence that the company can finally move forward.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry about the company’s size, the risk of construction overruns, and the need for a strong LNG market to justify the investment.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from neutral to mildly hopeful after the FID announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no public buzz, which is a good sign for a surprise play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the warrant exercise and the resulting capital injection.
Official construction start on the Sea Lion terminal.
Any partnership or supply‑agreement announcement that locks in LNG buyers.
A rise in global LNG spot prices that makes the project more attractive.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals: a breakout above the most recent low with a volume spike, confirmation of construction start, and any positive regulatory news.  These would be the green lights for a mean‑reversion move.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery from a deep drop.  APEX compression suggests a tight range that could snap once the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company has just pulled in fresh capital and secured a project milestone.  If the price can rebound to roughly halfway between its deepest trough and prior highs, the upside could be on the order of 4‑5 × the current level.  The historical performance of deep‑crash signals supports a high‑reward payoff.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still small, the LNG market is volatile, and construction delays or cost overruns could stall the project.  A flat or further decline would leave the stock near its current valuation with little upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the current trading level; upside potential is roughly 4‑5 × that level, giving a risk‑reward ratio of about 4:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break above the latest low with volume confirming the move.  Allocate 5 % of the portfolio, set a stop 10 % below the entry, and target a 200 % gain.  Use a trailing stop after the first 50 % gain to lock in profits.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the warrant exercise is fully announced and construction starts.  Monitor volume at the support level; a spike here is a strong entry signal.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.  The setup remains bullish barring major regulatory setbacks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, fresh equity, and a secured project milestone create a strong contrarian case for a 4‑to‑5‑fold rebound once the catalysts activate.
